output.root_cause: Upstream backfill on participation_fact_enriched created duplicate anon_id + experiment_id combinations, causing join fanout in random_sample CTE

decision_log.decision: Root cause is upstream backfill creating duplicates
decision_log.rationale: Unique key duplicate check found 1,247 duplicate keys in participation_fact_enriched. These duplicates cause the random_sample CTE join to fan out, resulting in 436,168 extra rows that violate the unique constraint on gpm_repeat_buyer_dim.
alternatives_considered: Join logic error — but the SQL is correct, the data is wrong

tool_calls.input: SELECT anon_id, experiment_id, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM participation_fact_enriched GROUP BY 1, 2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 LIMIT 10
SELECT MAX(CREATED_AT), COUNT(*) FROM participation_fact_enriched WHERE CREATED_AT >= DATEADD(day, -1, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
